title: Removing Regulatory Barriers to Affordable Home Construction
summary: Executive Order 14394, issued on March 13, 2026, aims to address the rising costs of housing by reducing regulatory barriers to home construction. The order mandates a review and revision of federal regulations related to water bodies, stormwater management, and permitting processes under the Clean Water Act. It also directs various federal agencies, including those for Housing and Urban Development, Commerce, Transportation, Energy, Agriculture, and the EPA, to reform or eliminate burdensome rules and programs that hinder residential development and affordability. Key areas of focus include streamlining permitting, reforming energy and water efficiency standards, and promoting best practices for state and local governments to expedite construction and reduce costs. Additionally, the order seeks to align Opportunity Zone tax incentives with housing construction initiatives and explore coordination with New Markets Tax Credits to boost single-family home development in targeted areas.
